item.author: nickytonline

item.title: 让我们弄清楚一件事：代码由人类阅读，由机器解释 | Let's get one thing straight: Code is read by humans, interpreted by machines
item.description:
无论您的代码多么雄辩，或者您如何将一堆代码变成单行代码都无关紧要。 如果您是唯一可以阅读代码的人，请让其他人去找经理或执行官......因为现在这是他们的问题。

拼写。 出去。 您的。 代码。

使其可读。 添加注释，将其抽象为读起来像句子的函数。

因为归根结底，人类必须维护它。 机器解释它。 因此，只要逻辑合理，就可以轻松愉快地阅读。



原文:

It doesn't matter how eloquent your code is or how you turned a bunch of code into a one-liner. If you are the only one who can read the code, have someone else go find a manager or executive...because it's their problem now.

Spell. Out. Your. Code.

Make it readable. Add comments, abstract it out into functions that read like a sentence.

Because at the end of the day, humans have to maintain it. Machines interpret it. So as long as the logic is sound, make it easy and pleasant to read.
item.author: sublimegeek

item.title: 我是如何（有机地）在 20 天内将 Twitter 机器人帐户从 0 增加到 750 个关注者的。 | How I (organically) grew a Twitter bot account from 0 to 750 followers in 20 days.
item.description:
好吧，我大大低估了可能性。 我在短短 21 天内就获得了 750 名关注者！

美化这个！ 是一个简单的推特机器人，可以为推文截取漂亮的截图。 要使用它，我只需在任何推文下提及@poet_this screenshot ，它就会回复。

早些时候，该机器人由https://poet.so提供支持。 这是一个 selenium 脚本，它的效率非常非常低，而且托管成本非常高。 所以，我创建了自己的自定义图像生成器来做同样的事情。

由于我是使用 Python Image Library 完成的，因此非常具有挑战性，我必须想出一些有趣的算法，例如，根据推文本身的行数垂直扩展推文模板。 现在，我有了合理的托管成本和足够好的脚本，可以制作成 Twitter 机器人。

但为什么？

将近一年以来，我一直在发展自己的 Twitter 帐户（3 年前创建的）。 在这 3 年里，我有 150 位了不起的追随者、朋友和故事要讲。
但是，你知道，这是一个很小的数字，不是吗？ 发展一个推特账户是极其困难的……或者是吗？ 我想。

我不知道。 我只是想要一个挑战，在那里我设法做一些我认为不可能的事情。

跳进去，我不知道该怎么做。 我的意思是，我知道使用它的人越多，它就会得到越多的宣传。 但是如何传播呢？

这也是我超级超级筋疲力尽的时候。 我没有任何编码的动力，我真的无事可做（我不看电影、电视节目，也不和朋友出去）。

如何？

这是它的时间表：
第 1 天到第 5 天：30 位关注者。 只有我在 Twitter 上到处使用这个机器人。 人们注意到，人们跟随。 但我落后于我的目标。

增长开始急剧放缓，我几乎放弃了。

但在第 5 天，我在 Elon Musk 的推文上使用了这个机器人。 幸运的是，在他发布推文后 10 秒（我真的很想截图分享给朋友哈哈）
天哪！ 我现在有个窍门。 只需打开 Elon musk 的通知，（lmao）就可以像其他人一样使用该机器人。 这对于传播知名度来说是惊人的。

该机器人现在获得了很多很多忠实用户。 将在每个帖子上使用该机器人的用户。 到第 10 天，我已经有大约 150 个追随者。

我现在有信心我可能会在 30 天内达到目标。 所以我将机器人的 about me 改为 30（增长再次开始放缓，我认为 50 是一个更现实的数字）。

到第 15 天，我有大约 300 名追随者。 现在，我不知道到底发生了什么，也许其他人在一条非常受欢迎的推文上使用了该机器人，但人们开始大量支持挑战。 我收到了电子邮件、DM 等等 - 很快，我醒来时就有 400 名追随者（大约在第 17 天）。 从那时起，该机器人每天都获得（几乎）100 个关注者。

今天是第21天。 它刚刚吸引了 750 名追随者，并且几乎达到了 800 人！ 帮助我在 30 天内获得 1000 名关注者！

挑战

我也面临很多挑战。 就像，高使用率意味着托管成本也上升了。 由于bot是图像生成，图像生成很多，我不得不关闭它一段时间以节省成本。 每当我在笔记本电脑上观看视频或放松时，我都会打开 Twitter 的通知并收听 ping。 然后在我自己的电脑上使用IMGEN脚本并手动发送结果图像。 是的...

每当有人以错误的方式使用机器人时，我都会手动向他们发送有关正确用法的消息，或者有时只是手动发送屏幕截图。

但这是可以理解的。

你可以如何提供帮助。

首先，给个关注！ 这将有助于它在不到 30 天内达到 1000 名关注者。

你也可以在经济上支持我，所以我不必让我的父母支付托管费用https://ko-fi.com/dhravya

此外，该机器人是开源的（然而，API 还不是开源的。仍在使用实际图像生成的 API 并且它有很多错误）

留个⭐


用漂亮的推文截图回复，由 beautify.dhravya.dev 提供支持

 git clone https://github.com/dhravya/poet-this.git

 cd poet-this
pip install -r requirements.txt

该项目在 MIT 许可下获得许可

显示您的支持

给一个如果你喜欢这个项目

自述文件使用Dhravya Shah 的 README 生成器






原文:

On 28th April, I published this blog, challenging myself to grow a twitter bot account to 1000 followers in 100 days. Well, I HUGELY underestimated the possibilities. I managed to get to 750 followers in just 21 days!

Beautify This! is a simple twitter bot that takes beautiful screenshots of tweets. To use it, all I had to do is mention @poet_this screenshot under any tweet and it will reply.

Earlier, the bot was powered by https://poet.so. And it was a selenium script that was really, really inefficient and super costly to host. So, I created my own custom image generator to do the same.

Since I did it using Python Image Library, it was quite challenging and I had to come up with interesting algorithms to, for example, vertically extend the tweet template based on the number of lines of the tweet itself. Now, I had reasonable hosting costs and a good enough script to be made into a twitter bot.

But why?

I've been growing my own twitter account since almost a year now (created it 3 years ago). In all these 3 years, I got 150 amazing followers, friends and stories to tell.
But well, you know, that's kinda a small number, isn't it? It is extremely difficult to grow a twitter account ... or is it? I thought.

I don't know. I just wanted a challenge where I manage to do something I thought was impossible.

Jumping in, I had no idea how to do this. I mean, I knew that more the people use it, the more publicity it will get. But how to spread the word?

This was also the time when I was super, super burnt out. I didn't have any motivation to code and I really had nothing to do (I don't watch movies, TV shows, I don't go out with friends).

How?

Here's a timeline of how it went:
Day 1 to 5 : 30 followers. It was just me using the bot everywhere on twitter. People noticed, people followed. But I was behind my goal.

The growth started to drastically slow down, I almost gave up.

But on day 5, I used the bot on an Elon Musk's Tweet. Luckily, it was literally 10 seconds after he posted the tweet (i genuinely wanted a screenshot to share to friends lol)
And HOLY SHIT! 5 followers, 10 followers, by the end of the day, I already had 70 followers! I had a trick now. Just turn on the notifications for Elon musk and (lmao) use the bot like anyone else would. This was amazing to spread visibility.

The bot got many, many loyal users now. Users who would use the bot on every one of their posts. by the 10th day, I already had about 150 followers.

I was now confident that I can probably hit the mark in 30 days. So I changed the about me of the bot to 30 (growth started slowing down again and I though 50 is a more realistic number) at this point.

by the 15th day, I had about 300 followers. Now, I don't know what the hell happened, maybe someone else used the bot on a very popular tweet, but people started supporting the challenge a LOT. I got emails, DMs, and what not - soon enough, I woke up to 400 followers (on about the 17th day). Since then, every single day, the bot has been getting (almost) 100 followers.

Today is the 21th day. It just hit 750 followers, and almost gonna hit 800! Help me get to 1000 followers in ... 30 DAYS!

Challenges

I also faced a ton of challenges. Like, the high usage meant that hosting costs also went up. Since the bot is image generation, a lot of image generation, I have to shut it down for some time to save costs. Whenever I'm on the laptop watching videos or relaxing, I turned on the notifications for twitter and listened to pings. And then used the IMGEN script on my own computer and manually sent the result image. Yeah...

Whenever someone used the bot in a wrong way, I manually send them messages on the correct usage, or sometimes just sent the screenshot manually.

Twitter flagged and muted many of the bot tweets, even though it's a "good" bot and has the automated tag. But it's understandable.

How you can help.

First of all, give a follow! https://twitter.com/poet_this. this will help it reach 1000 followers in less than 30 days.

You can also financially support me, so I don't have to ask my parents to pay for the hosting https://ko-fi.com/dhravya

Also, the bot is open source (The API, however, isn't open source yet. Still working on the API where the actual image generation happens and it has a lot of bugs)

Leave a ⭐


Replies with a beautiful screenshot of the tweet, powered by beautify.dhravya.dev 

git clone https://github.com/dhravya/poet-this.git

cd poet-this
pip install -r requirements.txt

This project is licensed under the MIT license

Show your support

Leave a ⭐ if you like this project

Readme made with 💖 using README Generator by Dhravya Shah

item.title: 这周你赢了什么？ | What was your win this week?
item.description:
格雷西·格雷戈里（她/她）

发表于嘿！

回顾过去的一周，你为完成的事情感到自豪吗？

所有的胜利都很重要——无论大小🎉

开始一个新项目

  • 修复一个棘手的错误
  • 获得一个新的小工具......或任何其他可能引发快乐的东西❤️

    • 周末愉快！



    原文:

    Gracie Gregory (she/her)

    Posted on May 20

    Hey there!

    Looking back on this past week, what was something you were proud of accomplishing?

    All wins count — big or small 🎉

    Examples of 'wins' include:

    • Starting a new project
    • Fixing a tricky bug
    • Getting a new gadget... or whatever else might spark joy ❤️

    Have a wonderful weekend!

item.title: 分享 ️Love ️ — 你最喜欢 DEV 上的 #gamedev 帖子是什么？ | Share the ️Love ️— What's your favorite #gamedev post on DEV?
item.description:
迈克尔·塔灵顿（他/他）

发表于我已经开始了一个名为“分享爱”的新系列，我鼓励你们把你们最喜欢的帖子放在一个特定的标签下，并@提及作者。 为什么？ 因为这是一种在一个地方收集大量精彩帖子的有趣方式，它为人们提供了一个互相呼喊的平台。 如果您被标记，请考虑将其转发给网站上的另一位作者——分享爱，得到它。 您最喜欢在#gamedev 下分享的帖子是什么？

所以，我想听听， #gamedev下大家最喜欢分享的帖子是什么？

当你分享他们的作品时，请考虑给作者一个@mention，因为这可能会给他们带来感觉。 注意：您可以通过编写以下语法将帖子嵌入到评论中。

{% embed dev.to/username/name-of-post %}

好的，现在是时候寻找您想要突出显示的最喜欢的#gamedev帖子了！ 分享爱！ 克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— May 16 5月16日



原文:

Michael Tharrington (he/him)

Posted on May 20

I've started up a new series called "Share the Love" where I'm encouraging y'all to drop your favorite post under a particular tag and @mention the author. Why? Cause it's a fun way to collect a lot of great posts in one spot and it gives folks a platform to give each other shoutouts. If you get tagged in, consider paying it forward to another author on the site — share the love, get it. 🙌

What's your favorite post shared under #gamedev?

So, I like to hear, what are everybody's favorite posts shared under #gamedev?

Please consider giving the author an @mention when ya share their work as it's likely gonna give them the feel-goods. 😀

Note: you can embed a post into a comment by writing the following syntax.

{% embed dev.to/username/name-of-post %}

Alright, now time to hunt down that favorite #gamedev post you'd like to highlight! Share the love! ❤️

Cristian Fernando - May 16
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 使用新 Java 运行时的云函数 | Cloud Functions Using the New Java Runtime
item.description:
在本文中，我们将了解如何使用Java 运行时编写 Cloud Functions。 如果您是 Android 开发人员，您基本上可以构建您的应用程序并编写任何必要的云功能，而无需学习任何新语言！ 如果缺少任何内容，您可以使用您喜欢的后端语言扩展 Appwrite。

在我们开始之前，有几个先决条件。 如果您已经设置了先决条件，则可以跳到以下部分。

为了跟进，您需要事先做一些事情。

如果您还没有设置 Appwrite 实例，您可以按照入门指南快速启动和运行。 您可以在 DigitalOcean 上的一键安装或使用 Docker 的手动安装之间进行选择。

服务器启动并运行后，转到服务器公共 IP 地址上的 Appwrite 仪表板（或本地安装，如果您在本地安装）并创建一个新用户帐户。

我们将在本练习中使用 Appwrite CLI，因为它使过程超级简单。 如果你安装了 Node.js，安装命令很简单

npm install -g appwrite-cli

如果你不喜欢npm ，我们有许多安装选项，你可以在CLI 的入门指南中找到

一切准备就绪，我们现在可以开始了！ 使用以下命令登录到 Appwrite CLI，并使用我们在设置 Appwrite 服务器时使用的凭据进行登录。

 appwrite login ? Enter your email test @test.com ? Enter your password ******** ✓ Success

接下来，我们需要创建一个新的 Appwrite 项目来使用。 我们可以使用以下命令进行设置

appwrite init project ? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project ? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project ✓ Success

您可以为您的项目指定您选择的任何名称。 在这种情况下，我将坚持使用默认值。 您会注意到当前目录中有一个新的appwrite.json文件，其中存储了有关您的项目的所有信息。

是时候开始编写我们的函数了！ 但是等等，我们不需要从头开始！ 为您的函数命名并选择Java 17.0运行时。 这将在您的项目中创建一个新的 Appwrite 函数并设置所有必要的样板代码。 随意检查在functions/java-example目录中创建的文件。 您会发现 CLI 创建了一个简单的 Java 函数，该函数返回一条非常重要的 JSON 消息

{ "areDevelopersAwesome" : true
}

在我们继续修改函数之前，让我们部署它以了解从初始化函数到部署它的端到端工作流程。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 ) ℹ Info Deploying function java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )

在此期间，将安装所有函数的依赖项，并为您的函数生成一个.jar文件。

您可以前往 Appwrite Dashboard 跟踪部署进度。

部署完成后，您可以通过单击立即执行按钮来执行您的功能。

第一次执行导致我们称之为冷启动。 本质上这是第一次创建运行时，因此需要更长的时间。 如您所见，第一次执行大约需要 450毫秒，后续执行大约需要4 毫秒。 现在我们已经了解了完整的工作流程，我们可以开始修改代码并编写一些很酷的函数。

正如标题所示，我们将编写一个云函数来回答数学中最基本的问题之一。

给定的数字是奇数还是偶数？ 我知道你在想什么……太简单了！ 为什么我需要一个云功能？ 我可以写

class Main { public static void main ( String [ ] args ) { int x = 12 ; System . out . println ( x % 2 == 0 ); // true System . out . println ( ( x / 2 ) * 2 == x ); // true System . out . println ( ( x & 1 ) == 0 ); // true System . out . println ( ( x >> 1 ) << 1 == x ); // true } }

可是等等！ 我们正在将此提升到一个新的水平。 我们将使用目前最复杂且编写良好的 API 之一来决定我们的数字是偶数还是奇数！ 撇开玩笑不谈，这个示例旨在说明如何从 Cloud Function 进行 API 调用，这将使您能够构建任何您想要的东西。

首先，在您喜欢的 IDE 中打开java-example文件夹。 让我们从一个空白画布开始，清理我们的src/Index.java文件以

import java.util.Map ; import java.util.HashMap ; public RuntimeResponse main ( RuntimeRequest req , RuntimeResponse res ) throws Exception { Map < String , Object > data = new HashMap <>(); data . put ( "message" , "Hello World!" ); return res . json ( data ); }

为了更轻松地处理 JSON 对象，我们将使用流行的gson库。 在deps.gradle文件中包含以下依赖项。

 dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0' }

接下来，让我们编写一些代码来读取和解析作为参数发送给该函数的数字。

 import java.util.Map ; import java.util.HashMap ; import com.google.gson.Gson ; final Gson gson = new Gson (); public RuntimeResponse main ( RuntimeRequest req , RuntimeResponse res ) throws Exception { String payloadString = req . getPayload () == null || req . getPayload (). isEmpty () ? "{}" : req . getPayload (); Map < String , Object > payload = gson . fromJson ( payloadString , Map . class ); String number = "2" ; if ( payload . containsKey ( "number" ) && payload . get ( "number" ) != null ) { number = payload . get ( "number" ). toString (); } Map < String , Object > data = new HashMap <>(); data . put ( "message" , "Hello World!" ); return res . json ( data ); }

让我们回顾一下我们刚刚编写的代码。 从函数文档中，我们看到payload可以通过request对象获得，并且是一个 JSON 字符串，需要转换为 JSON 对象才能进一步解析。 如果payload为空，我们将其替换为空的 JSON 对象。

然后我们检索传递给函数的number ，如果它为空，则默认为数字2 。 我们还导入gson库并创建它的一个实例。

接下来，让我们使用本机 HTTP 客户端调用 API。

 import java.util.Map ; import java.util.HashMap ; import java.net.HttpURLConnection ; import java.net.URL ; import java.io.BufferedReader ; import java.io.InputStreamReader ; import com.google.gson.Gson ; final Gson gson = new Gson (); public RuntimeResponse main ( RuntimeRequest req , RuntimeResponse res ) throws Exception { String payloadString = req . getPayload () == null || req . getPayload (). isEmpty () ? "{}" : req . getPayload (); Map < String , Object > payload = gson . fromJson ( payloadString , Map . class ); String number = "2" ; if ( payload . containsKey ( "number" ) && payload . get ( "number" ) != null ) { number = payload . get ( "number" ). toString (); } URL url = new URL ( "https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/" + number ); HttpURLConnection con = ( HttpURLConnection ) url . openConnection (); con . setRequestMethod ( "GET" ); con . getResponseCode (); BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader ( new InputStreamReader ( con . getInputStream ())); String inputLine ; StringBuilder responseString = new StringBuilder (); while (( inputLine = in . readLine ()) != null ) { responseString . append ( inputLine ); } in . close (); con . disconnect (); Map < String , Object > response = gson . fromJson ( responseString . toString (), Map . class ); return res . json ( response ); }

惊人的！ 我们都准备好测试我们的功能了！ 如果你还记得这个练习，我们需要做的就是部署我们的功能。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 ) ℹ Info Deploying function java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )

您现在可以前往 Appwrite 控制台并使用以下有效负载执行该函数。

转到“日志”选项卡以检查该功能的状态和响应。

完美的！ 看起来我们已经设法解决了数学中最紧迫的问题之一！ 更不用说，该 API 还带有许多古怪的广告！

这使我们结束了本教程。 随意根据自己的喜好修改函数并使用 API。 如果您遇到困难或需要任何帮助，我们随时为您提供帮助。 只需前往我们Discord 服务器上的#support频道😊

这里有一些方便的链接以获取更多信息



原文:

One of the highlights of Appwrite’s latest release is the addition of four new Cloud Function runtimes! Java, Kotlin, .NET and C++ are now a part of our ever growing list of runtimes! 🤯

In this article, we’ll take a look at writing Cloud Functions using the Java runtime. If you’re an Android developer, you could essentially build your app and write any necessary Cloud Functions without the need to learn any new language! 🤩

Appwrite is open source backend-as-a-service that abstracts all the complexity involved in building a modern application by providing you with a set of REST APIs for your core backend needs. Appwrite handles user authentication and authorization, realtime databases, file storage, cloud functions, webhooks and much more! If there is anything missing, you can extend Appwrite using your favorite backend language.

📝 Prerequisites

Before we get started, there are a couple of prerequisites. If you have the prerequisites setup already, you can skip to the following section.

In order to follow along, you’ll need a few things beforehand.

If you haven’t set up an Appwrite instance yet, you can follow the getting started guides to get up and running quickly. You can choose between One-click installations on DigitalOcean or Manual installations with Docker.

TL;DR - It's just a single command to install Appwrite

docker run -it --rm \ --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ --volume "$(pwd)"/appwrite:/usr/src/code/appwrite:rw \ --entrypoint="install" \
    appwrite/appwrite:0.14.2

Once your server is up and running, head over to the Appwrite Dashboard on your server’s public IP address ( or localhost if you installed locally ) and create a new user account.

We’ll use the Appwrite CLI during this exercise as it makes the process super simple. If you have Node.js installed, the installation command is a simple

npm install -g appwrite-cli

If npm is not your thing, we have numerous installation options you can find in the getting started guide for the CLI

🏁 Getting Started

With everything set up, we can now begin! Login to the Appwrite CLI using the following command and use the credentials we used when setting up the Appwrite server to login.

appwrite login
? Enter your email test@test.com
? Enter your password ********
✓ Success

Next, we need to create a new Appwrite project to work with. We can use the following command to set it up

appwrite init project
? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project
? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project
✓ Success

You can give your project any name of your choice. I’m going to stick to the defaults in this case. You’ll notice a new appwrite.json file in the current directory which stores all the information about your project.

It’s time to start writing our function! But wait, we don't need to start from scratch! The CLI can setup all the boilerplate for us using the following command

appwrite init function
? What would you like to name your function? java-example
? What runtime would you like to use? Java (java-17.0)
✓ Success

Give your function a name and choose the Java 17.0 runtime. This will create a new Appwrite Function in your project and set up all the boilerplate code necessary. Feel free to examine the files created in the functions/java-example directory. You’ll find the CLI created a simple Java function that returns a very important JSON message

{ "areDevelopersAwesome":true
}

Before we go ahead and modify the function, let’s deploy it to get a feel of the end-to-end workflow from initializing the function to deploying it.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? java-example (6286f6905af1e032e934)
ℹ Info Deploying function java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )

The appwrite deploy function command packages your source code, uploads it to the Appwrite server and initiates the build process. During this time, all the function’s dependencies are installed and a .jar file is generated for your function.

You can head over to your Appwrite Dashboard to track the progress of your deployment.

Once the deployment completes, you can execute your function by clicking the Execute Now button.

The first execution results in what we call a Cold Start. Essentially this is the first time a runtime is created, so it takes a bit longer. As you can see, the first execution took about 450ms and subsequent executions were around 4ms. Now that we have an idea of the complete workflow, we can start tinkering with the code and write some cool functions.

🧮 Answering the Most Fundamental Question in Mathematics

As the title suggests we’re going to write a cloud function to answer one of the most fundamental questions in Mathematics.

Is the given number odd or even? I know what you’re thinking… It’s so easy! Why do I need a Cloud Function for it? I could just write

class Main { public static void main(String[ ] args) { int x = 12; System.out.println( x%2 == 0 ); // true System.out.println( (x/2) * 2 == x ); // true System.out.println( (x & 1) == 0 ); // true System.out.println( ( x >> 1) << 1 == x ); // true }
}

But wait! We’re taking this to the next level. We’re going to decide if our number is even or odd using one of the most sophisticated and well written APIs out there! The isEven API.

Jokes aside, this example aims to illustrate how you can make API calls from your Cloud Function which will enable you to build anything you’d like.

Firstly, open the java-example folder in your favorite IDE. Let’s start with a blank canvas and clean up our src/Index.java file to

import java.util.Map;
import java.util.HashMap; public RuntimeResponse main(RuntimeRequest req, RuntimeResponse res) throws Exception { Map<String, Object> data = new HashMap<>(); data.put("message", "Hello World!"); return res.json(data);
}

In order to deal with JSON objects more easily, we’ll make use of the popular gson library. Include the following dependency in the deps.gradle file.

dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0'
}

Next, let’s write some code to read and parse a number sent as an argument to this function.

import java.util.Map;
import java.util.HashMap;
import com.google.gson.Gson; final Gson gson = new Gson(); public RuntimeResponse main(RuntimeRequest req, RuntimeResponse res) throws Exception { String payloadString = req.getPayload() == null || req.getPayload().isEmpty() ? "{}" : req.getPayload(); Map<String, Object> payload = gson.fromJson(payloadString, Map.class); String number = "2"; if (payload.containsKey("number") && payload.get("number") != null) { number = payload.get("number").toString(); } Map<String, Object> data = new HashMap<>(); data.put("message", "Hello World!"); return res.json(data);
}

Let’s go over the code we just wrote. From the functions documentation, we see that the payload is available through the request object and is a JSON string that needs to be converted to a JSON object to parse further. If the payload is empty, we replace it with an empty JSON object.

We then retrieve the number that was passed to the function and default to the number 2 in case it’s empty. We also import the gson library and create an instance of it.

Next, let’s make the call to the API using the native HTTP client.

import java.util.Map;
import java.util.HashMap;
import java.net.HttpURLConnection;
import java.net.URL;
import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;
import com.google.gson.Gson; final Gson gson = new Gson(); public RuntimeResponse main(RuntimeRequest req, RuntimeResponse res) throws Exception { String payloadString = req.getPayload() == null || req.getPayload().isEmpty() ? "{}" : req.getPayload(); Map<String, Object> payload = gson.fromJson(payloadString, Map.class); String number = "2"; if (payload.containsKey("number") && payload.get("number") != null) { number = payload.get("number").toString(); } URL url = new URL("https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/" + number); HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection(); con.setRequestMethod("GET"); con.getResponseCode(); BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream())); String inputLine; StringBuilder responseString = new StringBuilder(); while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) { responseString.append(inputLine); } in.close(); con.disconnect(); Map<String, Object> response = gson.fromJson(responseString.toString(), Map.class); return res.json(response);
}

Awesome! We’re all set to test our function! If you remember the drill, all we need to do is deploy our function.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? java-example (6286f6905af1e032e934)
ℹ Info Deploying function java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed java-example ( 6286f6905af1e032e934 )

You can now head over to the Appwrite console and execute the function with the following payload.

Head over to the Logs tab to check the status and response from the function.

Perfect! Looks like we’ve managed to solve one of the most pressing problems in Mathematics after all! Not to mention, the API also comes with a host of quirky Ads!

And that brings us to the end of this tutorial. Feel free to modify the function to your liking and play around with the API. If you’re stuck or need any help, we’re always here to help. Simply head over to the #support channels on our Discord Server 😊

📚 Resources

Here are some handy links for more information
item.author: christyjacob4

item.title: 在 VS Code 中安装 Prettier 的 Ruby 插件 | Installing the Ruby Plugin for Prettier in VS Code
item.description:
我发现它在学习 JavaScript 和 React 时非常有帮助，既可以提高我的格式和打字效率，也可以教会我格式良好的代码实际上是什么样的。

当我开始学习 Ruby 时，我非常想念 prettier 的易用性，最终决定为 Prettier 安装 Ruby 插件以尝试获得相同的功能。 然而，安装证明比我预期的要复杂，我不得不从 TA Wills Blake 那里获得一些额外的帮助。 我们一起想通了。 说明如下。

该插件应该全局安装，以便在 VS Code 中打开的任何新项目都可以自动使用它。 但是， Prettier提供的插件安装说明含糊不清，似乎暗示插件必须作为每个新项目的依赖项安装。 这需要创建一个 gemfile，添加依赖项等等......不，我们想做他们在Prettier Plugins 页面上描述的事情。 正如该页面上所说， “如果您将插件安装在 prettier 所在的同一 node_modules 目录中，则会自动加载插件”。 所以让我们找到那个目录。

要遵循的步骤：

  1. 确保你已经在 VS Code 中安装了 Prettier 扩展
  2. 通过cd进入命令行导航到您的主目录，或打开一个新的 CLI 窗口
  3. 输入ls -a显示所有文件夹，包括隐藏文件夹
  4. 如果您在使用 WSL 的 Windows 上，您应该会看到一个.vscode-server目录。 如果你在 macOS 上，它应该只是.vscode 。 应该有一个名为esbenp.prettier-vscode-<version>的目录（即，我的目录是esbenp.prettier-vscode-9.5.0 ）。 在 CLI 中输入npm install --save-dev prettier @prettier/plugin-ruby
  5. 等待它完成安装，然后重新启动 VS Code。

您现在应该可以在 VS Code 中右键单击任何 ruby​​ 文档并选择“格式化文档”！ 默认情况下，您也可以在保存时格式化。 如果没有，您可以使用以下步骤导航到 VS Code 设置文件：

  1. 按住 ctrl（或 macOS 的 cmd）+ shift + P
  2. 输入settings.json并输入
  3. 向下滚动 json 文件。 您应该已经有一个对象指定 prettier 作为 javascript 代码的默认格式化程序：
 "[javascript]" : { "editor.defaultFormatter" : "esbenp.prettier-vscode"
} ,

在其下方添加以下内容：

 "[ruby]" : { "editor.defaultFormatter" : "esbenp.prettier-vscode" , "editor.formatOnSave" : true
} ,

你有它！



原文:

Prettier is the premier code formatter extension for front-end languages in VS Code, at least if you go by the number of downloads in the extension marketplace. I found it to be extremely helpful while learning JavaScript and React, both to improve my formatting and typing efficiency as well as to teach me what well-formatted code actually looks like.

When I started to learn Ruby, I was sorely missing the ease of use of prettier and finally decided to install the Ruby plugin for Prettier to try to get the same functionality. However, the installation proved trickier than I expected and I had to recruit some additional help from TA Wills Blake. Together, we figured it out. The instructions are below.

The plugin should be installed globally so that it is automatically available to any new projects opened in VS Code. However, the instructions provided by Prettier for installing the plugin are vague and seem to imply that the plugin must be installed as a dependency with each new project. This requires creating a gemfile, adding in the dependency, etc.....nah, we want to do what they're describing on the Prettier Plugins page. As it says on that page, "plugins are automatically loaded if you have them installed in the same node_modules directory where prettier is located". So let's find that directory.

Steps to follow:

  1. Make sure that you have already installed the Prettier extension in VS Code
  2. Navigate to your home directory by cd into the command line, or opening a new CLI window
  3. Enter ls -a to show all folders, including hidden ones
  4. If you are on Windows using WSL, you should see a .vscode-server directory. If you're on macOS, it should be simply .vscode. cd into the directory.
  5. cd into extensions.
  6. There should be a directory titled esbenp.prettier-vscode-<version> (ie, mine was esbenp.prettier-vscode-9.5.0). cd into it.
  7. Enter npm install --save-dev prettier @prettier/plugin-ruby in the CLI
  8. Wait for it to finish installing, then restart VS Code.

You should now be able to right-click in any ruby document in VS Code and select "format document"! You may also be able to format on save by default. If not, you can navigate to the VS Code Settings file using the following steps:

  1. Hold down ctrl (or cmd for macOS) + shift + P
  2. Type settings.json and enter
  3. Scroll down the json file. You should already have an object specifying prettier as the default formatter for javascript code: 
"[javascript]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode"
},

Add the following below it:

"[ruby]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode", "editor.formatOnSave": true
},

There you have it!
item.author: drayeleo

item.title: 我终于完成了我的投资组合 | I finally completed my portfolio
item.description:
尚泰 P.

发表于所以我创建了我的投资组合。 我很难想出一个设计模板来让我的作品集看起来如何。 我知道我想要一些简单而不是太复杂的东西，因为我还是个新手。 因此，我使用了 Freecodecamp video中的主题。 即使我没有使用确切的设计，你认为雇主会因为我没有提出自己的设计和代码而认为这是负面的吗？ 让我知道你们的想法！

扎基·阿卜杜拉赫曼—— Apr 18 4月18日

Dumb Down Demistifying Dev - Dumb Down Demistifying Dev - May 11 5月11日



原文:

Chantae P.

Posted on May 20

So I have created my portfolio. I had a hard time coming up with a design template for how I wanted my portfolio to look. I know I wanted something simple and not too complicated since I'm still a newb. So instead, I used the theme from Freecodecamp video. Even though I didn't use the exact design, do you think employers will see this a negative since I didn't come up with my own design and code? Let me know what you guys think!

P.S. The resume is still work in progress.

Zaky Abdurrahman - Apr 18

Dumb Down Demistifying Dev - May 11
item.author: taepal467

item.title: 使用新 Kotlin 运行时的云函数 | Cloud Functions Using the New Kotlin Runtime
item.description:
在本文中，我们将了解如何使用 Kotlin 运行时编写 Cloud Functions。 如果您是 Android 开发人员，您基本上可以构建您的应用程序并编写任何必要的云功能，而无需学习任何新语言！ 如果缺少任何内容，您可以使用您喜欢的后端语言扩展 Appwrite。

在我们开始之前，有几个先决条件。 如果您已经设置了先决条件，则可以跳到以下部分。

为了跟进，您需要事先做一些事情。

如果您还没有设置 Appwrite 实例，您可以按照入门指南快速启动和运行。 您可以在 DigitalOcean 上的一键安装或使用 Docker 的手动安装之间进行选择。

服务器启动并运行后，转到服务器公共 IP 地址上的 Appwrite 仪表板（或本地安装，如果您在本地安装）并创建一个新用户帐户。

我们将在本练习中使用 Appwrite CLI，因为它使过程超级简单。 如果你安装了 Node.js，安装命令很简单

npm install -g appwrite-cli

如果你不喜欢npm ，我们有许多安装选项，你可以在CLI 的入门指南中找到。

一切准备就绪，我们现在可以开始了！ 使用以下命令登录到 Appwrite CLI，并使用我们在设置 Appwrite 服务器时使用的凭据进行登录。

 appwrite login ? Enter your email test @test.com ? Enter your password ******** ✓ Success

接下来，我们需要创建一个新的 Appwrite 项目来使用。 我们可以使用以下命令进行设置

appwrite init project ? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project ? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project ✓ Success

您可以为您的项目指定您选择的任何名称。 在这种情况下，我将坚持使用默认值。 您会注意到当前目录中有一个新的appwrite.json文件，其中存储了有关您的项目的所有信息。

是时候开始编写我们的函数了！ 但是等等，我们不需要从头开始！ 为您的函数命名并选择Kotlin 1.6运行时。 这将在您的项目中创建一个新的 Appwrite 函数并设置所有必要的样板代码。 随意检查在functions/kotlin-example目录中创建的文件。 您会发现 CLI 创建了一个简单的 Kotlin 函数，该函数返回一条非常重要的 JSON 消息

{ "areDevelopersAwesome" : true
}

在我们继续修改函数之前，让我们部署它以了解从初始化函数到部署它的端到端工作流程。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

在此期间，将安装所有函数的依赖项，并为您的函数生成一个.jar文件。

您可以前往 Appwrite Dashboard 跟踪部署进度。

部署完成后，您可以通过单击立即执行按钮来执行您的功能。

第一次执行导致我们称之为冷启动。 本质上这是第一次创建运行时，因此需要更长的时间。 如您所见，第一次执行大约需要 450毫秒，后续执行大约需要4 毫秒。 现在我们已经了解了完整的工作流程，我们可以开始修改代码并编写一些很酷的函数。

正如标题所示，我们将编写一个云函数来回答数学中最基本的问题之一。

给定的数字是奇数还是偶数？ 我知道你在想什么……太简单了！ 为什么我需要一个云功能？ 我可以写

fun main () { val x = 12 println ( x % 2 == 0 ) // true println ( ( x / 2 ) * 2 == x ) // true println ( x and 1 == 0 ) // true println ( ( x shr 1 ) shl 1 == x ) // true }

可是等等！ 我们正在将此提升到一个新的水平。 我们将使用目前最复杂且编写良好的 API 之一来决定我们的数字是偶数还是奇数！ 撇开玩笑不谈，这个示例旨在说明如何从 Cloud Function 进行 API 调用，这将使您能够构建任何您想要的东西。

首先，在您喜欢的 IDE 中打开kotlin-example文件夹。 让我们从一个空白画布开始，清理我们的src/Index.kt文件以

@Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { return res . json ( mapOf ( "message" to "Hello, World!" )) }

为了更轻松地处理 JSON 对象，我们将使用流行的[gson](https://github.com/google/gson)库。 在deps.gradle文件中包含以下依赖项。

 dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0' }

接下来，让我们编写一些代码来读取和解析作为参数发送给该函数的数字。

 import com.google.gson.Gson private val gson = Gson () @Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( req . payload . ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap :: class . java ) val number = payload [ "number" ] ?: 2 return res . json ( mapOf ( "message" to "Hello, World!" )) }

让我们回顾一下我们刚刚编写的代码。 从函数文档中，我们看到payload可通过request对象获得，并且是一个 JSON 字符串，需要将其转换为 JSON 对象才能进一步解析。 如果payload为空，我们将其替换为空的 JSON 对象。

然后我们检索传递给函数的number ，如果它为空，则默认为数字2 。 我们还导入gson库并创建它的一个实例。

接下来，让我们使用本机 HTTP 客户端调用 API。

 import com.google.gson.Gson import java.io.BufferedReader import java.io.InputStreamReader import java.net.HttpURLConnection import java.net.URL private val gson = Gson () @Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( req . payload . ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap :: class . java ) val number = payload [ "number" ] ?: 2 val url = URL ( "https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/$number" ) val con = ( url . openConnection () as HttpURLConnection ). apply { requestMethod = "GET" responseCode } val responseString = buildString { BufferedReader ( InputStreamReader ( con . inputStream )). useLines { lines -> lines . forEach { append ( it ) } } } con . disconnect () val response = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( responseString , MutableMap :: class . java ) return res . json ( response ) }

惊人的！ 我们都准备好测试我们的功能了！ 如果你还记得这个练习，我们需要做的就是部署我们的功能。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

您现在可以前往 Appwrite 控制台并使用以下有效负载执行该函数。

转到“日志”选项卡以检查该功能的状态和响应。

完美的！ 看起来我们已经设法解决了数学中最紧迫的问题之一！ 更不用说，该 API 还带有许多古怪的广告！ 喜欢这个买老人👴

这使我们结束了本教程。 随意根据自己的喜好修改函数并使用 API。 如果您遇到困难或需要任何帮助，我们随时为您提供帮助。 只需前往我们Discord 服务器上的#support频道😊

这里有一些方便的链接以获取更多信息



原文:

One of the highlights of Appwrite’s latest release is the addition of four new Cloud Function runtimes! Java, Kotlin, .NET and C++ are now a part of our ever growing list of runtimes! 🤯

In this article, we’ll take a look at writing Cloud Functions using the Kotlin runtime. If you’re an Android developer, you could essentially build your app and write any necessary Cloud Functions without the need to learn any new language! 🤩

Appwrite is open source backend-as-a-service that abstracts all the complexity involved in building a modern application by providing you with a set of REST APIs for your core backend needs. Appwrite handles user authentication and authorization, realtime databases, file storage, cloud functions, webhooks and much more! If there is anything missing, you can extend Appwrite using your favorite backend language.

📝 Prerequisites

Before we get started, there are a couple of prerequisites. If you have the prerequisites setup already, you can skip to the following section.

In order to follow along, you’ll need a few things beforehand.

If you haven’t set up an Appwrite instance yet, you can follow the getting started guides to get up and running quickly. You can choose between One-click installations on DigitalOcean or Manual installations with Docker.

TL;DR - It's just a single command to install Appwrite

docker run -it --rm \ --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ --volume "$(pwd)"/appwrite:/usr/src/code/appwrite:rw \ --entrypoint="install" \
    appwrite/appwrite:0.14.2

Once your server is up and running, head over to the Appwrite Dashboard on your server’s public IP address ( or localhost if you installed locally ) and create a new user account.

We’ll use the Appwrite CLI during this exercise as it makes the process super simple. If you have Node.js installed, the installation command is a simple

npm install -g appwrite-cli

If npm is not your thing, we have numerous installation options you can find in the getting started guide for the CLI.

🏁 Getting Started

With everything set up, we can now begin! Login to the Appwrite CLI using the following command and use the credentials we used when setting up the Appwrite server to login.

appwrite login
? Enter your email test@test.com
? Enter your password ********
✓ Success

Next, we need to create a new Appwrite project to work with. We can use the following command to set it up

appwrite init project
? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project
? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project
✓ Success

You can give your project any name of your choice. I’m going to stick to the defaults in this case. You’ll notice a new appwrite.json file in the current directory which stores all the information about your project.

It’s time to start writing our function! But wait, we don't need to start from scratch! The CLI can setup all the boilerplate for us using the following command

 appwrite init function
? What would you like to name your function? kotlin-example
? What runtime would you like to use? Kotlin (kotlin-1.6)
✓ Success

Give your function a name and choose the Kotlin 1.6 runtime. This will create a new Appwrite Function in your project and set up all the boilerplate code necessary. Feel free to examine the files created in the functions/kotlin-example directory. You’ll find the CLI created a simple Kotlin function that returns a very important JSON message

{ "areDevelopersAwesome":true
}

Before we go ahead and modify the function, let’s deploy it to get a feel of the end-to-end workflow from initializing the function to deploying it.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example (62863924adaff7c88a10)
ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

The appwrite deploy function command packages your source code, uploads it to the Appwrite server and initiates the build process. During this time, all the function’s dependencies are installed and a .jar file is generated for your function.

You can head over to your Appwrite Dashboard to track the progress of your deployment.

Once the deployment completes, you can execute your function by clicking the Execute Now button.

The first execution results in what we call a Cold Start. Essentially this is the first time a runtime is created, so it takes a bit longer. As you can see, the first execution took about 450ms and subsequent executions were around 4ms. Now that we have an idea of the complete workflow, we can start tinkering with the code and write some cool functions.

🧮 Answering the Most Fundamental Question in Mathematics

As the title suggests we’re going to write a cloud function to answer one of the most fundamental questions in Mathematics.

Is the given number odd or even? I know what you’re thinking… It’s so easy! Why do I need a Cloud Function for it? I could just write

fun main() { val x = 12 println( x%2 == 0 ) // true println( (x/2) * 2 == x ) // true println( x and 1 == 0 ) // true println( ( x shr 1) shl 1 == x ) // true
}

But wait! We’re taking this to the next level. We’re going to decide if our number is even or odd using one of the most sophisticated and well written APIs out there! The isEven API.

Jokes aside, this example aims to illustrate how you can make API calls from your Cloud Function which will enable you to build anything you’d like.

Firstly, open the kotlin-example folder in your favorite IDE. Let’s start with a blank canvas and clean up our src/Index.kt file to

@Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { return res.json(mapOf( "message" to "Hello, World!" ))
}

In order to deal with JSON objects more easily, we’ll make use of the popular [gson](https://github.com/google/gson) library. Include the following dependency in the deps.gradle file.

dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0'
}

Next, let’s write some code to read and parse a number sent as an argument to this function.

import com.google.gson.Gson private val gson = Gson() @Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( req.payload.ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap::class.java ) val number = payload["number"] ?: 2 return res.json(mapOf( "message" to "Hello, World!" ))
}

Let’s go over the code we just wrote. From the functions documentation, we see that the payload is available through the request object and is a JSON string that needs to be converted to a JSON object to parse further. If the payload is empty, we replace it with an empty JSON object.

We then retrieve the number that was passed to the function and default to the number 2 in case it’s empty. We also import the gson library and create an instance of it.

Next, let’s make the call to the API using the native HTTP client.

import com.google.gson.Gson
import java.io.BufferedReader
import java.io.InputStreamReader
import java.net.HttpURLConnection
import java.net.URL private val gson = Gson() @Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( req.payload.ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap::class.java ) val number = payload["number"] ?: 2 val url = URL("https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/$number") val con = (url.openConnection() as HttpURLConnection).apply { requestMethod = "GET" responseCode } val responseString = buildString { BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(con.inputStream)).useLines { lines -> lines.forEach { append(it) } } } con.disconnect() val response = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( responseString, MutableMap::class.java ) return res.json(response)
}

Awesome! We’re all set to test our function! If you remember the drill, all we need to do is deploy our function.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example (62863924adaff7c88a10)
ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

You can now head over to the Appwrite console and execute the function with the following payload.

Head over to the Logs tab to check the status and response from the function.

Perfect! Looks like we’ve managed to solve one of the most pressing problems in Mathematics after all! Not to mention, the API also comes with a host of quirky Ads! Like this one to purchase old people 👴

And that brings us to the end of this tutorial. Feel free to modify the function to your liking and play around with the API. If you’re stuck or need any help, we’re always here to help. Simply head over to the #support channels on our Discord Server 😊

📚 Resources

Here are some handy links for more information

item.title: Appwrite 社区报告 #7 | Appwrite Community Report #7
item.description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于来自 Appwrite 团队的问候！ 我们带着每周更新回来了。 本周我们有一些惊人的公告，更多细节在里面展开。 在我们深入研究报告之前，我们向社区和 GitHub 上的 22k 观星者表示感谢！ 我们在 YouTube 上也有 Appwrite，更多的学习资源和更多的好处。 请继续关注到最后！ 将您的申请提交至 -

在 0.14 版本中，我们集成了来自社区的一些功能请求，努力消除现有错误并引入了新功能：

  • 修复了使用 CRON 作业执行的计划 Cloud Functions 执行
    修复了缺少的运行时图标
    修复了谷歌 OAuth
    修复了主机名设置为“localhost”时生成的证书
    修复了覆盖默认环境变量的安装
    可以在 [这里] 找到更多详细信息。（ https://github.com/appwrite/appwrite/blob/master/CHANGES.md ）

    修复失败的 Zoom OAuth

  • 在 Appwrite 中添加 Rust 平台支持

为 Appwrite 创建 Helm 图表，你怎么看？

使用 Hugo 和 Appwrite 构建博客网站 -

  • 用 Appwrite 构建了一些很酷的解决方案？ 一定要把它们放在[这里]。（ https://github.com/appwrite/awesome-appwrite ）
  • 社区成员@devenrathod为我们制作了一个 GitHub stargazer 应用程序。



    • 原文:

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on May 20

    Greetings from the Appwrite team! 👋

    We are back with our weekly updates. This week we have some amazing announcements, more details unfold inside. 👀

    Before we dive right into the report, we extend our gratitude to the community and 22k star-gazers on GitHub! 🙏🏽

    TLDR; Appwrite 0.14 is the new talk of the open-source town, as we introduced some cloud functions, some new features and more. We also have Appwrite on YouTube, more learning resources and more goodness. Stay tuned till the end! 😉

    📢 What’s new

    • Appwrite 0.14 with 11 Cloud Function Runtimes 🤩 https://github.com/appwrite/appwrite/discussions/3240
    • 3 weeks into the OSS fund, and we have already received some amazing responses. 🤩 If you would love some support, Appwrite is here to help! Submit your application to -

      Appwrite provides web and mobile developers with a set of easy-to-use and integrate REST APIs to manage their core backend needs.

    ✅ Issues solved

    With the 0.14 release we have integrated some feature requests from the community, worked to remove existing bugs and introduced new features:

    • Fixed scheduled Cloud Functions execution with CRON job
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed missing runtime icons
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed Google OAuth
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed certificate generation when hostname was set to 'localhost'
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed installation overriding default environment variables
      🔗 Link to PR

    More details can be found [here].(https://github.com/appwrite/appwrite/blob/master/CHANGES.md)

    ⚒️ What we’re currently working on

    • Appwrite is working towards v.10 and the cloud launch, which is why we are launching the State of the Appwrite Community survey to gather inputs and feedback.

    P.S - 5 random survey submissions will be selected for a $100 Amazon Gift Card or equivalent Appwrite swag credits.

    • Fixing a failing Zoom OAuth
    • Adding Rust platform support in Appwrite

    🗣 Discussions

    • Creating a Helm chart for Appwrite, what do you think about it?

    🔗 Join discussion here

    📘 Resources

    • Building a blogging site with Hugo and Appwrite -
    • Built some cool solutions with Appwrite? Be sure to drop them [here].(https://github.com/appwrite/awesome-appwrite)
    • Appwrite is on YouTube, did you check our installation video yet?
    • 30 days of Appwrite is now updated, have you checked them out already?

    🙌 Credits

    • Community member @devenrathod made a GitHub stargazer app for us.

item.title: 毕业后和模拟技术面试 | Post Graduation and Mock Technical Interview
item.description:
训练营是一次疯狂的旅程，我很高兴能够完成并开始我的新职业。 我想谈谈我对 Flatiron 训练营和 Skilled 模拟技术面试的看法。

大多数人可能想知道的是训练营值得吗？ 简而言之，我会说是的。 我没有编码知识或经验，所以我不知道从哪里开始自学。 我喜欢这些课程是如何相互建立的，以及您如何看到这些信息如何以不同的语言使用，而不是他们教授的语言。 回想起来，我希望我能亲自参加或更结构化的训练营。 由于当时的日程安排，我没能做到，但是看了前几期的讲座后，信息以一种更容易理解的方式呈现出来。 如果您像我一样参加自定进度的课程，请选择以前的同伙，边看边看所有视频。 我真的很喜欢 Laura 和 Eri 的同伙。 我看到很多自定进度的学生真的很挣扎，如果不是我的决心，我不知道我会如何完成课程。 当你觉得自己一个人时，很难保持动力。 学习小组也很有帮助。 大多数时候，只是人们就他们的最终项目提出问题，但即使我不熟悉这个概念，我也从中学到了很多。 我会记录学习小组，当我遇到课程中的主题时，我会回去再次观看学习小组的那个部分。 达斯汀（Dustin）是自定进度的队列负责人，他非常乐于助人，并且经常现场编写代码并谈论特定主题，观看起来非常棒。 对于每个部分的最终评估，我总是很紧张，但我总是第一次就通过了。 我对这个成就感觉非常好。

这是我对训练营的建设性批评。 有些课程真的很难理解。 一些实验室完全没有说明，并且有非常奇怪的测试，没有好的反馈。 例如，有几次因为我将一个类命名为不同的名称，我的测试不会通过，但它不会说它找不到该类，而是会给出一个不同的奇怪错误。 我最喜欢的 React 部分已经过时了。 最近开始在 hooks 上添加额外的内容，但除非你知道去寻找它，否则你将使用非常过时的信息进行技术面试。 幸运的是，我正在观看 Laura 的同类视频，她教钩子。 她还给了我很多很好的资源供我使用，也可以让我自己了解它们。 我在我的最终项目中使用了钩子，现在对钩子很着迷。

说到 hooks 和 React，毕业后你会得到一个令牌，可以通过 Skilled 进行模拟技术面试。 我决定参加 React 评估。 我以优异的成绩通过了，这是一次很棒的面试。 问题部分主要询问有关 React 的广泛主题。 像这样的问题：什么是 JSX？ 什么是钩子以及如何使用它们？ 你如何存储状态？ 解释类与功能组件。 有没有更好的方法来存储状态，你会推荐使用什么库？ 什么是useEffect以及它接受的依赖数组是什么？ 如果您将该数组留空怎么办？

对于实时编码，我必须制作一个增加和减少的计数器按钮。 然后我不得不对其进行限制，使其不能低于 0 或超过 10。 对于下一部分，我必须调用 API 并显示来自 API 的信息。 我用于这两个的唯一钩子是useState和useEffect 。 我当然犯了一些小错误，但能够在没有太多提示的情况下快速修复它们。 总的来说，我为自己能够在其他人面前编写代码并能够回答他们所有的问题而感到自豪。

以下是我在技术面试中得到的反馈：“Dorthy 也是一个很棒的人选！在整个面试过程中，她非常专业和友善。我们以简单的介绍开始了面试，我们讨论了经验和未来的努力. 我们首先通过讨论高级概念性问题开始面试过程，她在这些问题上做得很好！她能够清楚地回答每个问题。接下来，我们进入了一个涉及多轮代码挑战的代码挑战“

说了这么多，我真的很高兴我把这种信念的飞跃带入了新的职业生涯。 这是一个令人谦卑和有益的过程。 无论如何，这不是一段轻松的旅程，但我真的很努力。 同样很棒的是其他工程师的支持程度。 我接触过的每一个人都非常乐于助人和善良。 没有秘密，每个人都想互相帮助，这真是令人耳目一新。 那是我从来没有真正喜欢过的关于木偶制造的事情，人们不想告诉你的所有商业秘密。 我总是与其他人分享我知道的任何信息。 甚至我工作多年的地方也不会告诉我一些材料和技术。 甚至一位老板也会从一些材料上取下标签，并会在下班后工作以保密他的商业机密。 我认为这是该行业遭受如此多痛苦的部分原因。 在一个如此养育的新环境中呼吸新鲜空气。 我现在正在寻找工作，并希望尽快找到一些东西。



原文:

On March 24th I graduated the Flatiron Full Stack Dev bootcamp. It was a wild ride being the bootcamp and I am so excited to be done and starting my new career. I wanted to give my thoughts on the Flatiron bootcamp, and the Skilled mock technical interview.

What probably most people are wondering is was the bootcamp worth it? In short, I would say yes. I had no coding knowledge or experience going in so I would have had no idea where to start teaching myself. I liked how the lessons built on each other and how you could see how the information could be used in different languages other than the ones they teach. In retrospect, I wish I would have done the in person or more structured bootcamp. I wasn't able to due to my schedule at the time, but having watched the lectures from previous cohorts, the information was presented in a more digestible way. If you do the self-paced course like I did, pick a previous cohort and watch all the videos as you go. I really liked Laura's and Eri's cohort the best. I watched a lot of self-paced students really struggle through and if it wasn't for my determination, I don't know how I would have every completed the course. It is tough to stay motivated when you feel like you are all by yourself. The study groups are also really helpful. Most of the time it is just people asking about questions on their final project, but I learned a lot from that even when I wasn't familiar with the concept. I would record the study group and when I came across the topics in the course, I would go back and watch that section of the study group again. Dustin, who is the self-paced cohort lead, was so helpful and would often live code and talk about a particular subject and it was so great to watch. I was always so nervous for the final assessments for each section, but I always passed on my first go. I feel really great about that accomplishment.

Here is my constructive criticism on the bootcamp. Some of the lessons are really hard to understand. Some of the labs have absolutely no instructions and have really weird tests with no good feedback. For example, a few times because I named a class something different, my tests wouldn't pass but it wouldn't say that it couldn’t find the class but would give a different weird error. The React section, which was my favorite, is very outdated. The recently started adding additional content on hooks, but unless you know to seek it out, you will be going into technical interviews with very outdated information. Luckily I was watching Laura's cohort videos and she teaches hooks. She also gave a lot of good resources for me to use to also teach myself about them. I used hooks in my final project and am obsessed with hooks now.

Speaking of hooks and React, after graduation you get a token to have a mock technical interview through Skilled. I decided to take the React assessment. I passed with flying colors and it was a great interview. Hyung Kim was my assessor and was so nice and put me at ease immediately. The question section asked mostly about broad topics about React. Questions like: What is JSX? What are hooks and how do you use them? How do you store state? Explain class versus functional components. Is there a better way to store state and what library would you recommend using? What is useEffect and what is the dependency array it takes in? What if you leave that array empty?

For the live coding I had to make a counter button that increased and decreased. I then had to put a limit on it so it couldn't go below 0 or passed ten. For the next section, I had to call an API and display information from the API. The only hooks I used for both of these were useState and useEffect. I of course made a few tiny mistakes but was able to quickly fix them without a lot of hints. Overall I am really proud of myself for being able to live code in front of other people and be able to answer all of their questions.

Here is the feedback I got about my technical interview: "Dorthy was a great candidate to talk too! She was very professional and nice during the entire interview process. We started the interview with brief introductions of each other where we discussed experience and future endeavors. We first started the interview process by discussing high level conceptual questions in which she did a great job answering on! She was able to answer each and everyone of those questions clearly. Next, we moved onto a code challenge in which involved multiple rounds of questions. She did a very good job of walking me through the challenge question as well. Well on her way to be a successful engineer!"

With all of this said, I am really happy I took this leap of faith into a new career. It has been a humbling and rewarding process. It wasn't an easy journey by any means but I worked really hard. The thing that has also been so great is how supportive other engineers have been. Every single person I have interacted with has been so helpful and kind. There are no secrets, everyone wants to help each other and that is so refreshing. That was something I never really liked about puppet fabrication were all the trade secrets that people didn't want to tell you. I always shared any information I knew with other people. Even places I worked at for years wouldn't tell me some of the materials and techniques. Even one boss would take the labels off of some of the materials and would do work after hours to keep his trade secrets to himself. I think that is part of the reason the industry is suffering so much. It is such a breath of fresh air to be in a new environment that is so nurturing. I am now looking for jobs and hoping to land something soon.
item.author: dotnotation

item.title: `this`、`new` 和 OOP 简介 | Introduction to `this`, `new` & OOP
item.description:
大家好~我来解释一下什么是面向对象编程，以及它是如何在 JavaScript 中实现的。 我们将回顾 OOP 的理论并讨论关键字“this”和“new”在代码中的含义。

在最高级别，我们可以将 OOP 描述为尝试将一组数据和功能打包到一个我们可以使用的项目中。 这个想法是信息和处理该信息的功能（通过操作它，记录它，删除它等）应该是一个独立的包。 这是一个以数据为中心的模型。

这消除了几个问题，例如需要可能相互冲突的全局变量，以及代码在文件中的分布使其难以阅读和遵循。

在 JavaScript 中，我们试图将所有数据和函数放入的这个包将是一个普通对象。

关键字this和new向开发人员表明我们正在使用面向对象编程。 它们表明我们正在创建和使用打包的信息集。

对于许多开发人员来说，这是一个巨大的痛苦，即使是那些有多年经验的人。 它在 JavaScript 中的行为与在其他语言中的行为不同。

我们将使用这个一般概念作为我们的指导方针：在 JavaScript 中，函数内部的this旨在引用该函数作为其属性的对象。

它旨在将开发人员的注意力吸引到一个对象上。 使用时， this关键字基本上是在喊出这个函数应该处理的主要对象是this 。

检查“这个”

在 JavaScript 中，每个函数都在某个对象上调用，因此 this 在每个函数中都有一个值。 让我们从一个普通的函数调用开始。

尽管fn看起来好像是自由调用的，没有绑定到任何对象，但它实际上是由 JavaScript 引擎作为全局对象的属性存储的。 在浏览器中，全局对象是window 。 如果我们打开浏览器的控制台并在上面的代码块中输入以下代码，我们可以验证fn确实是window的属性。

 console.log(window.fn === fn); // -> true

我们知道相等运算符在处理函数或其他对象时会检查引用，因此我们可以确定fn与window.fn完全相同。 当我们省略window时，JavaScript 会隐式地将其输入到我们的代码中。

这意味着this ？ 请记住，根据我们的指南，函数中的this应该表示正在调用该函数的对象。 在简单地调用fn()的情况下，这正是我们所看到的。

 function fn() { console.log(this); } fn(); // -> Window {frames: Window, postMessage: ƒ, …}

我们看到this等于window 。 因此，它遵循我们表示该函数被调用的对象的准则。

像我们在这里所做的那样自由调用函数的行为被称为自由函数调用。 为什么在这里没用

正如我们所看到的，让this等于全局对象并不是很有用。 如果我们想从函数内部为全局对象设置一个变量（我们真的不应该这样做），我们可以完全省略var 、 let和const关键字。

如果我们想访问全局范围内的东西，我们仍然不需要这个。 如果我们确实需要访问全局范围，我们总是可以使用window或当前环境中的任何全局对象。

理想情况下， this应该类似于自由调用的函数内部的undefined 。 不幸的是，事实并非如此，我们必须处理它在代码可读性和理解方面引起的问题。

我们已经介绍了this的无用情况。 我们永远不会以这种方式使用它，但我们应该意识到这个功能存在的事实。 让我们继续讨论如何this OOP 中实际使用它。

方法调用

作为对象属性的函数称为方法。 从技术上讲，所有函数都是方法，因为自由函数隐含地变成了全局对象的方法。 让我们看一个函数，它显式地是我们定义的某个对象的方法。

 const obj = { str: 'Hello!', fn: function() { console.log(this); } }; obj.fn(); // -> { str: 'Hello!', fn: [Function: fn] }

该函数是obj的属性。 当我们调用obj.fn()时，我们看到函数内部的this等于对象obj 。 这再次符合我们的准则，即函数中的 this 应该是函数所在的对象。

假设我们想在obj上有一个计数器。 我们想要一个obj上的方法来增加计数器。

 const obj = { counter: 0, incrementCounter: function() { this.counter++; this.logCounter(); }, logCounter: function() { console.log(this.counter); } }; obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 1 obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 2 obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 3

我们开始看到面向对象编程的症结所在。 该对象被制成一个独立的项目，应该使用它自己的内部方法进行操作。

它表明我们打算使用 OOP，并且函数返回的对象将包含一组打包的数据，并且可能包含与该数据一起使用的方法。

关键字也保留了它的原始含义——当我们使用new时，我们总是返回一个全新的、新构造的对象，并带有一个全新的引用。 该对象在函数内部存在。

 function fn() { } console.log(fn()); // -> undefined console.log(new fn()); // -> fn {}

即使我们在fn中没有代码，当使用new调用时，它仍然会自动返回一个新对象。 我们将在下一课中讨论这些规则和细微差别。 现在，我们看到new与 OOP 中对象的生成密切相关。

请注意，由于上面的第 5 行，我们看到的项目已记录到控制台，它会注销fn {} 。 如果我们记录一个使用new创建的对象，控制台会记录创建该对象的函数的名称，然后是该对象。

这篇简短的面向对象理论应该让你做好准备
在接下来的几节课中。 我们将在下一篇博客中看到“新”规则。

希望这个博客对你的现实生活有所帮助！



原文:

Hello~ I am going to explain what object-oriented programming is and how it's implemented in JavaScript. We'll go over the theory of OOP and discuss what the keywords 'this' and 'new' mean in regards to code.

A Gentle Introduction to OOP

At the highest level, we can describe OOP as an attempt to package a set of data and functionality into one item that we can work with. The idea is that information and functions that work on that information (by manipulating it, logging it, deleting it, etc.) should be a self-contained package. It’s a data-centric model.

This eliminates several problems, such as the need for global variables that might conflict with one another, and the spread of code across a file that makes it difficult to read and follow.

In JavaScript, this package that we’re trying to put all of our data and functions in will be a plain object.

The keywords this and new are signals to a developer that we are using object oriented programming. They indicate that we are creating and working with packaged sets of information.

The idea of ‘this’

this is closely tied to OOP. It is a source of great pain for many developers, even those with years of experience. It behaves differently in JavaScript than it does in other languages.

We’ll use this general idea as our guideline: In JavaScript, this inside a function is meant to reference the object that the function is a property of.

The rules in JavaScript are a bit odd, but understanding the point of this and why it exists in programming languages should ease the explanation. It’s meant to draw the developer’s attention to one single object. When used, the this keyword is basically shouting out that the main object this function is supposed to work on is this.

Examining ‘this’

In JavaScript, every function is called on some object and therefore this has a value in every function. Let’s start with a normal function call.

Useless this in Free Function Invocation

function fn() {
    // do something...
}

fn();

Although it seems as if fn is called freely, not bound to any object, it’s actually stored by the JavaScript engine as a property on the global object. In a browser, the global object is window. If we open up our browser’s console and type in the code in the block above with the following line, we can verify that fn is indeed a property of window.

console.log(window.fn === fn); // -> true

We know that the equality operator, when working on functions or other objects, checks the reference, so we can be certain that fn is exactly the same as window.fn. When we omit window, JavaScript implicitly enters it into our code.

What does this mean for this? Remember that according to our guideline, this in a function is supposed to represent the object that the function is being called on. In the case of simply calling fn(), that’s exactly what we see.

function fn() {
    console.log(this);
}

fn(); // -> Window {frames: Window, postMessage: ƒ, …}

We see that this is equal to window. So it follows our guideline of representing the object the function is being called upon.

The act of calling a function freely as we’re doing here is referred to as a free function invocation. The value of this in an FFI is the global object.

Why it’s Useless Here

As we can see, having this equal the global object isn’t very useful. If we want to set a variable on the global object from inside a function (something we should really never be doing), we can just omit the var, let, and const keywords entirely.

If we want to access something in the global scope, we still don’t need this. If we ever did need access to the global scope we could always use window or whatever the global object is in the current environment.

Ideally, this should be something like undefined inside a function invoked freely. Unfortunately, it’s not, and we have to deal with the issues it causes in terms of code readability and understanding.

We’ve covered the useless case of this. We’ll never be using it this way, but we should be aware of the fact that this functionality exists. Let’s move on to how this is actually used in OOP.

Method Call

A function that is a property of an object is called a method. Technically, all functions are methods, as free functions are implicitly turned into methods of the global object. Let’s look at a function that is explicitly a method of some object that we define.

const obj = {
    str: 'Hello!',
    fn: function() {
        console.log(this);
    }
};

obj.fn(); // -> { str: 'Hello!', fn: [Function: fn] }

The function is a property of obj. We see that this inside the function when we invoke obj.fn() is equal to the object obj. This again meets our guideline that this in a function should be the object that the function is on.

Let’s say we want to have a counter on obj. We want a method on obj that will increment the counter.

const obj = {
    counter: 0,
    incrementCounter: function() {
        this.counter++;
        this.logCounter();
    },
    logCounter: function() {
        console.log(this.counter);
    }
};

obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 1
obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 2
obj.incrementCounter(); // -> 3

We’re starting to see the crux of object oriented programming. The object is made into a self-contained item which should be manipulated using its own internal methods.

Introduction to new

new is another keyword used in OOP. new is used to invoke a function in a different way than we’re used to. The point of new is to indicate that we want an object returned by the function.

It indicates that we intend to use OOP and that the object the function returns is going to contain a packaged set of data and maybe methods to go along with that data.

The keyword also retains its original meaning - when we use new, we’re always being returned a fresh, newly constructed object with a brand new reference. This object comes into existence inside the function.

function fn() {
}

console.log(fn()); // -> undefined
console.log(new fn()); // -> fn {}

Even though we have no code inside fn, when invoked with new, it still automatically returns a new object. We’ll go into these rules and nuances in the next lesson. For now, we see that new is closely tied to the generation of objects in OOP.

Note that the item we see logged to the console due to line 5 above logs out fn {}. If we log an object that was created using new, the console logs out the name of the function that created the object and then the object.

This short bit of object oriented theory should have you primed
for the next few lessons. We will see the Rules to 'new' in next blog.

Hope this blog would be helpful for your real-life!
item.author: topcode007

item.title: AlmaLinux 现已在 Oracle Cloud Marketplace 上可用 | AlmaLinux now available on Oracle Cloud Marketplace
item.description:
你知道甲骨文有云吗？ 是的，那个甲骨文。 这也很酷。

我们很高兴地宣布，AlmaLinux 映像现已在Oracle 的 OCI 市场上提供，适用于x86_64和aarch64架构。

全球数以千计的开发人员依靠 Oracle 技术和应用程序来推动他们的应用程序、项目和业务。 他们庞大的全球开发人员网络依靠 Oracle Cloud 来帮助他们构建一些非常了不起的东西。 如果您只想查看其中一些示例，请查看Pi Day中的一些内容。 这只是皮毛，因为许多企业客户依赖 Oracle 堆栈，以及越来越多的组织依赖 OCI 作为多云部署策略的一部分。 他们现在也可以访问他们应得的并且一直在要求的社区拥有和管理的、永远免费的企业级分发。

我们合作的另一个要素是我们与合作伙伴Ampere Computing的长期友谊。 毫不奇怪，它们是第一个“云原生”处理器。 我们要感谢他们的团队再次与我们合作，并提供了出色的技术指导和全方位的协作。

还要感谢 Oracle 云团队。 他们一直在努力与开源社区进行更多的互动，我们也很高兴能够与他们一起进行这种合作。 查看Oracle Cloud 免费层上的AlmaLinux，让我们知道您的想法。



原文:

Did you know that Oracle has a cloud? Yup, that Oracle. It's pretty cool too.

We're pleased to announce that AlmaLinux images are now available on Oracle's OCI Marketplace for both x86_64 and aarch64 architectures.

Thousands of developers around the world rely on Oracle technology and applications to fuel their applications, projects and businesses. Their massive, global developer network relies on Oracle Cloud to help them build some pretty amazing things. If you wanna see just some of these examples, check out some of the content from Pi Day. That's just scratching the surface, as many enterprise customers rely on the Oracle stack, as well as increasing number of organizations relying on OCI as part of a multi-cloud deployment strategy. They too now have access to the community owned and governed, forever-free enterprise-grade distribution they deserve and have been asking for.

Another element of our collaboration is our long standing friendship with our partners Ampere Computing. Ampere are OCI's hardware provider for their A1 instance types, which are aarch64 architecture. Ampere has some of the fastest, highest performing processors on the planet and above all that, they're also more energy efficient. It's no surprise they are the first "Cloud Native" processor. We'd like to thank their team for work with us on this again and providing awesome technical guidance and just all around collaboration.

Thanks go out to the Oracle Cloud team as well. They have been working hard on engaging more with open source communities and we're glad to be able to work with them on this collaboration as well. Check out AlmaLinux on Oracle Cloud's Free Tier and let us know what you think.

item.title: 3 种更好的 pull request 描述的方法 | 3 ways to make better pull request descriptions
item.description:
在团队工作时，拉取请求（也称为合并请求）在 git 工作流程中至关重要。 如果您的项目具有 CI/CD 设置，通常需要合并以触发代码到生产环境。 这篇文章概述了使您的拉取请求描述更好的三种方法。

确保你清楚你的代码做了什么以及为什么它是必不可少的。 解释它的作用，并包括任何必要的东西来展示它的作用。 包括为什么让代码审查者知道拉取请求的目的。

拉取请求的内容和原因可能看起来很相似，但它们却大不相同。 拉取请求的内容解释了代码的作用，以及为什么定义了拉取请求的影响。

例子

我想提交一个更新 ESLint 的 PR。

 ## What This PR adds a new ESLint rule to adjust the import order ## Why It makes the code easier to read and easier to follow by organizing imports in a predictable way. It also groups imports by siblings and parents.

在上面的示例中，说明了代码的作用以及为什么说明了拉取请求的必要性。

在描述中提及审阅者应该如何测试拉取请求的工作方式。 明确说明代码审查员应该如何评估代码将使他们更容易。

例子

基于上面的例子：

 ## What This PR adds a new ESLint rule to adjust the import order ## Why It makes the code easier to read and easier to follow by organizing imports in a predictable way. It also groups imports by siblings and parents. ## How to test 1. Open a JavaScript file 2. Change the import order 3. Save file and see how it changed

上面的例子是一个很好的开始，但是提升它的一种方法是在这样的描述中实际给出代码之前和代码之后。

如果您有拉取请求的结构，您应该创建一个模板。 模板提供了拉取请求结构，因为它指导作者应该包括什么。 它还确保拉取请求的所有必要部分都在那里。

示例模板可能如下所示：

 ## What Describe what this PR does ## Why Describe the impact that this PR makes ## How to test Describe how to test that this PR does what it says it does ## Checklist - [ ] Ran all tests locally - [ ] There are no credentials stored in the code - [ ] Has this been signed off by PM or designers or any other stakeholders?

以下是在 GitHub、GitLab 和 BitBucket 上创建拉取或合并请求模板的链接。 我通过谷歌搜索“如何在 {platform} 上制作拉取请求模板”找到了这些，所以如果您的平台不在这里，那么您可能可以使用精确搜索找到它。

如果您对这篇文章有任何意见，请务必在 Twitter 上告诉我， @maeganwilson_并关注我。

☕ Support my articles by buying me a coffee ☕ 给我买杯咖啡来支持我的文章



原文:

A pull request, aka merge request, is crucial in the git workflow when working on a team. If your project has CI/CD setup, it is usually the piece that needs to be merged to trigger code to production. This post outlines three ways to make your pull request descriptions better.

1. What and Why

Make sure you are clear about what your code does and why it’s essential. Explain what it does and include whatever is necessary to show what it does. GitHub allows images and gifs in the pull request description, so if those would help illustrate you’re point, then do it. Including a why lets the code reviewer know the purpose of the pull request.

How does What and Why differentiate

The what and why of a pull request might seem similar, but they are quite different. The what of the pull request explains what the code does, and the why defines the pull request's impact.

Example

I want to submit a PR that updates ESLint.

## What

This PR adds a new ESLint rule to adjust the import order

## Why

It makes the code easier to read and easier to follow by organizing imports in a predictable way. It also groups imports by siblings and parents.

In the example above, the what says what the code does and the why explains how the pull request is necessary.

2. How to determine if this PR is good to merge

Mention how the reviewer is supposed to test the pull request works in the description. Explicitly stating how the code reviewer is supposed to evaluate the code will make it easier for them.

Example

Building on the above example:

## What

This PR adds a new ESLint rule to adjust the import order

## Why

It makes the code easier to read and easier to follow by organizing imports in a predictable way. It also groups imports by siblings and parents.

## How to test

1. Open a JavaScript file
2. Change the import order
3. Save file and see how it changed

The above example is a great start, but a way to take it up a level would be to actually give before code and after code in the description like this.

✉️ Get articles directly to your email.

3. Create a template

If you have a structure to your pull request, you should create a template. A template gives pull requests structure since it guides the author on what they should include. It also ensures that all the necessary pieces to the pull request are there.

A sample template might look like this:

## What

Describe what this PR does

## Why

Describe the impact that this PR makes

## How to test

Describe how to test that this PR does what it says it does

## Checklist

- [ ] Ran all tests locally
- [ ] There are no credentials stored in the code
- [ ] Has this been signed off by PM or designers or any other stakeholders?

Here are links to making a pull or merge request template on GitHub, GitLab, and BitBucket. I found these by googling “How to make pull request template on {platform}” so if your platform isn’t here, then you can probably find it using the exact search.

If you have any comments about this post, then make sure to let me know on Twitter, @maeganwilson_ and follow me there.

☕ Support my articles by buying me a coffee
